metadata.contractTitle: Statement of Work - Physician Diagnostic AI Rules Engine
metadata.pricingFull: Fixed price SOW with milestone-based invoicing; total fees USD 275,000 payable across five milestones upon acceptance or deemed acceptance of each milestone deliverable
metadata.emLevel: EM4 – Fixed Price (fixed-price engagement with committed milestones, deliverables, acceptance criteria, and budget ownership by CTIND; CTIND owns delivery schedule and is entitled to revise schedule and charge additional fees if ClientDBA causes delays)
metadata.billingCadence: Milestone-based invoicing upon acceptance or deemed acceptance of each milestone
metadata.terminationTerms: CTIND entitled to payment for all services performed up to effective date of termination including fees for accepted milestones, work in progress, and pre-approved investments and third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of ClientDBA
metadata.securityTerms: ClientDBA to provide security policies; formal penetration testing by certified third party excluded unless separately agreed; security contact to be made available by ClientDBA
metadata.scopeChangeTerms: Changes in scope, deliverables, acceptance criteria, assumptions, dependencies, and timelines require written change order or amendment signed by both parties; additional parameters, diagnostic categories, integrations, advanced AI/ML model development, production support, security certification, or regulatory support not expressly included shall be treated as change request
metadata.chargeableEvents: Delays in ClientDBA approvals, sample data, environments, licenses, access, security reviews, or clinical validation may impact delivery dates and fees; CTIND entitled to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees through change management process if milestone delivery delayed due to causes attributable to ClientDBA
metadata.exitObligations: M5 includes UAT support, deployment readiness package, release notes, known issue log, configuration guide, KT sessions, and final handover
metadata.executionEvidence: Signature blocks present on page 4 for authorized signatories from both ClientDBA and CTIND with fields for Name, Title, and Date; actual signatures not visible in uploaded contract

rows.deliverable: Complete project delivery
rows.measurement: Completion by Agreed Completion Date or mutually agreed extended date
rows.clause: The project under this SOW is expected to be completed by 31-Dec-2026 or any other extended date as mutually agreed between the parties
rows.valueAtRisk: Potential schedule extension and additional fees if completion delayed
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA timely provision of approvals, data, environments, access, and clinical validation
rows.remedy: CTIND entitled to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees through change management if delays attributable to ClientDBA
rows.recommendedAction: Establish clear governance for tracking ClientDBA dependencies and documenting delay causes to support schedule revision and additional fee claims
rows.evidence: The project under this SOW is expected to be completed by 31-Dec-2026 or any other extended date as mutually agreed between the parties
rows.reason: Completion date is stated as expected rather than committed; extension mechanism relies on mutual agreement which may create negotiation friction if delays occur

rows.deliverable: Medical diagnosis, treatment prescription, clinical decision-making, regulatory approvals, medical content approval, clinical validation, clinical safety sign-off
rows.clause: The tool shall not be treated as a standalone medical diagnosis, treatment prescription, or automated clinical decision-making system unless expressly agreed in a separate written agreement. Final diagnosis, patient management, care decisions, regulatory approvals, medical content approval, clinical validation, and clinical safety sign-off shall remain the responsibility of ClientDBA and its qualified clinical stakeholders.
rows.valueAtRisk: Potential liability exposure if tool is used for clinical decision-making without proper validation; regulatory non-compliance risk; patient safety risk
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA responsible for all clinical validation, regulatory approvals, medical content approval, and clinical safety sign-off
rows.exclusions: Medical diagnosis, treatment prescription, automated clinical decision-making, regulatory approvals, medical content approval, clinical validation, clinical safety sign-off excluded from CTIND scope
rows.recommendedAction: Obtain written confirmation from ClientDBA clinical and legal stakeholders acknowledging responsibility for clinical validation, regulatory compliance, and patient safety; ensure tool displays prominent disclaimers; document all clinical rule approvals with ClientDBA sign-off
rows.evidence: The tool shall not be treated as a standalone medical diagnosis, treatment prescription, or automated clinical decision-making system unless expressly agreed in a separate written agreement. Final diagnosis, patient management, care decisions, regulatory approvals, medical content approval, clinical validation, and clinical safety sign-off shall remain the responsibility of ClientDBA and its qualified clinical stakeholders.
rows.reason: AI-enabled diagnostic support tool creates material clinical, regulatory, and liability risk; scope explicitly excludes regulatory certification, clinical validation, and safety sign-off; unclear whether ClientDBA has acknowledged these responsibilities in writing or has qualified clinical resources to perform validation

rows.deliverable: Cloud infrastructure, third-party AI platforms, medical knowledge bases, LLM/API subscriptions, security tools, laboratory interfaces, EHR integrations, production monitoring tools
rows.clause: Procurement costs for cloud infrastructure, third-party AI platforms, medical knowledge bases, LLM/API subscriptions, security tools, laboratory interfaces, EHR integrations, and production monitoring tools are excluded unless specifically included in this SOW.
rows.valueAtRisk: Unbudgeted third-party costs may materially increase ClientDBA total cost of ownership
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA responsible for procuring and funding all third-party licenses, cloud infrastructure, AI platforms, and integration tools
rows.exclusions: All third-party procurement costs excluded from USD 275,000 fixed price
rows.recommendedAction: Provide ClientDBA with estimated third-party cost breakdown for cloud, AI/LLM APIs, security tools, and monitoring; obtain ClientDBA budget approval and procurement commitment before starting implementation phases
rows.evidence: Procurement costs for cloud infrastructure, third-party AI platforms, medical knowledge bases, LLM/API subscriptions, security tools, laboratory interfaces, EHR integrations, and production monitoring tools are excluded unless specifically included in this SOW.
rows.reason: Third-party costs for AI platforms, LLM APIs, cloud infrastructure, and security tools can be substantial and are explicitly excluded from fixed price; ClientDBA may not have budgeted for these costs; lack of cost transparency may cause budget overruns or project delays

rows.deliverable: Independent regulatory certification, SaMD/medical device classification support, legal advisory, HIPAA/GDPR advisory, formal penetration testing by certified third party, post-production managed services
rows.clause: Independent regulatory certification, SaMD/medical device classification support, legal advisory, HIPAA/GDPR advisory, formal penetration testing by a certified third party, and post-production managed services are excluded unless separately agreed.
rows.valueAtRisk: Regulatory non-compliance risk; security vulnerability risk; lack of post-production support may impact operational continuity
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA responsible for regulatory certification, medical device classification, HIPAA/GDPR compliance, penetration testing, and post-production support
rows.exclusions: Regulatory certification, SaMD/medical device classification, legal advisory, HIPAA/GDPR advisory, formal penetration testing, post-production managed services excluded
rows.recommendedAction: Clarify whether tool requires SaMD/medical device classification; if yes, engage regulatory consultant; conduct formal penetration testing before production; establish post-production support agreement covering defect remediation, security patches, and operational support
rows.evidence: Independent regulatory certification, SaMD/medical device classification support, legal advisory, HIPAA/GDPR advisory, formal penetration testing by a certified third party, and post-production managed services are excluded unless separately agreed.
rows.reason: Diagnostic AI tool may require regulatory classification as medical device or SaMD; HIPAA/GDPR compliance is critical for patient data; formal penetration testing is industry standard for healthcare applications; post-production support is not included which creates operational risk after handover

rows.deliverable: Five milestones per Annexure 1
rows.clause: If delivery of any milestone is delayed due to causes attributable to ClientDBA, CTIND will be entitled to revise its delivery schedule and charge additional fees to ClientDBA through the change management process.
rows.valueAtRisk: Schedule delays may trigger additional fees; lack of clear delay attribution process may cause disputes
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA timely provision of clinical rule owners, physician SMEs, product owner, security contact, infrastructure contact, UAT users, approved parameter list, reference ranges, sample datasets, clinical-rule documentation, environments, access, approvals
rows.remedy: CTIND entitled to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees through change management if delays attributable to ClientDBA
rows.recommendedAction: Establish clear process for documenting ClientDBA-caused delays with date, dependency, impact, and approval; obtain ClientDBA acknowledgment of delay attribution before invoking schedule revision or additional fees; track all dependency requests and response times
rows.evidence: If delivery of any milestone is delayed due to causes attributable to ClientDBA, CTIND will be entitled to revise its delivery schedule and charge additional fees to ClientDBA through the change management process.
rows.reason: Delay attribution and additional fee mechanism is stated but process for documenting and agreeing delay causes is not defined; may create disputes over whether delays are attributable to ClientDBA; additional fees are not capped

rows.deliverable: Sample blood test parameter datasets, reference ranges, clinical rules, diagnostic categories, physician review feedback
rows.clause: ClientDBA shall provide sample blood test parameter datasets, reference ranges, clinical rules, diagnostic categories, and physician review feedback required for implementation and testing.
rows.valueAtRisk: Lack of timely or complete clinical data may delay milestones and trigger additional fees
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA provision of sample datasets, reference ranges, clinical rules, diagnostic categories, physician feedback
rows.remedy: CTIND entitled to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees if delays attributable to ClientDBA
rows.recommendedAction: Define specific data and content delivery dates tied to milestone schedule; establish data quality and completeness criteria; obtain ClientDBA commitment to provide data at least 10 business days before each milestone due date
rows.evidence: ClientDBA shall provide sample blood test parameter datasets, reference ranges, clinical rules, diagnostic categories, and physician review feedback required for implementation and testing.
rows.reason: Clinical data and content provision is critical dependency but no delivery dates or quality criteria are specified; delays in data provision may cascade to milestone delays and additional fees

rows.deliverable: Medical logic, rule thresholds, clinical labels, disclaimers, user-facing suggested diagnostic text
rows.clause: ClientDBA clinical stakeholders shall review and approve all medical logic, rule thresholds, clinical labels, disclaimers, and user-facing suggested diagnostic text before production use.
rows.valueAtRisk: Lack of documented clinical approval creates liability risk; delays in clinical approval may delay milestones and production deployment
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA clinical stakeholders to review and approve all medical logic, rule thresholds, clinical labels, disclaimers, and diagnostic text
rows.recommendedAction: Establish formal clinical approval process with defined review cycle, approval authority, sign-off documentation, and turnaround time; obtain written clinical approval for all medical logic, rule thresholds, and diagnostic text before each milestone acceptance; maintain approval audit trail
rows.evidence: ClientDBA clinical stakeholders shall review and approve all medical logic, rule thresholds, clinical labels, disclaimers, and user-facing suggested diagnostic text before production use.
rows.reason: Clinical approval is critical for patient safety and liability protection but no approval process, turnaround time, or documentation requirements are defined; lack of documented clinical approval creates material liability risk for both parties; delays in clinical approval may cascade to milestone delays

rows.deliverable: Web-based application with configurable rules engine
rows.clause: Scope assumes a web-based application with a configurable rules engine and no live production EHR/LIS integration unless added through change control.
rows.valueAtRisk: Production utility may be limited without EHR/LIS integration; integration may require significant additional fees
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to request EHR/LIS integration through change control if required
rows.exclusions: Live production EHR/LIS integration excluded unless added through change control
rows.recommendedAction: Clarify ClientDBA production deployment plan and whether EHR/LIS integration is required; if integration is needed, initiate change request early to avoid deployment delays; provide integration cost and timeline estimate
rows.evidence: Scope assumes a web-based application with a configurable rules engine and no live production EHR/LIS integration unless added through change control.
rows.reason: Diagnostic tool may have limited production utility without EHR/LIS integration for automated data flow; integration is excluded from scope which may require significant additional investment and delay production deployment

rows.deliverable: Blood test parameters and diagnostic suggestion categories
rows.target: Agreed parameters and categories documented during discovery
rows.clause: The initial release will support agreed blood test parameters and diagnostic suggestion categories documented during discovery. Additional parameters, conditions, or integrations will be handled through change request.
rows.valueAtRisk: Limited initial coverage may require multiple change requests and additional fees to achieve production utility
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to define complete parameter and category list during discovery
rows.exclusions: Additional parameters, conditions, or integrations beyond discovery scope excluded
rows.remedy: Change request process for additional parameters, conditions, or integrations
rows.recommendedAction: Work with ClientDBA during M1 discovery to define comprehensive parameter and category list covering anticipated production use cases; document agreed scope clearly; provide cost and timeline estimate for adding parameters post-discovery
rows.evidence: The initial release will support agreed blood test parameters and diagnostic suggestion categories documented during discovery. Additional parameters, conditions, or integrations will be handled through change request.
rows.reason: Initial scope is limited to parameters and categories defined during discovery; production utility depends on comprehensive coverage; additional parameters require change requests and additional fees which may not be budgeted

rows.deliverable: Environments, APIs, infrastructure, test users, sample data, security policies, decision-makers
rows.clause: ClientDBA shall provide timely access to environments, APIs, infrastructure, test users, sample data, security policies, and decision-makers.
rows.valueAtRisk: Delays in environment or access provision may delay milestones and trigger additional fees
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA timely provision of environments, APIs, infrastructure, test users, sample data, security policies, decision-makers
rows.remedy: CTIND entitled to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees if delays attributable to ClientDBA
rows.recommendedAction: Define specific environment and access delivery dates tied to milestone schedule; establish access request and provisioning process with defined turnaround time; track all access requests and delays
rows.evidence: ClientDBA shall provide timely access to environments, APIs, infrastructure, test users, sample data, security policies, and decision-makers.
rows.reason: Timely access is critical dependency but no specific delivery dates or turnaround times are defined; delays in environment or access provision may cascade to milestone delays and additional fees

rows.deliverable: Each milestone deliverable
rows.target: Agreed requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria, test cases, approved rule configuration
rows.clause: Each milestone shall be assessed against agreed requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria, test cases, and approved rule configuration for that milestone.
rows.valueAtRisk: Lack of clear acceptance criteria may cause acceptance disputes and payment delays
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to agree acceptance criteria for each milestone
rows.recommendedAction: Document detailed acceptance criteria for each milestone during M1 discovery; obtain ClientDBA written approval of acceptance criteria before starting each milestone; include acceptance criteria in milestone delivery documentation
rows.evidence: Each milestone shall be assessed against agreed requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria, test cases, and approved rule configuration for that milestone.
rows.reason: Acceptance criteria must be mutually agreed but process and documentation requirements are not defined; lack of clear documented acceptance criteria may cause disputes during acceptance testing

rows.deliverable: Suggested diagnostic output
rows.target: Traceable to configured rules, input parameter values, applicable thresholds, rule version
rows.clause: Suggested diagnostic output must be traceable to configured rules, input parameter values, applicable thresholds, and rule version used at the time of generation.
rows.valueAtRisk: Lack of traceability may create regulatory compliance risk and limit clinical validation ability
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to verify traceability during acceptance testing
rows.recommendedAction: Implement comprehensive audit trail capturing rule version, input parameters, thresholds, output, timestamp, and user; provide traceability report for each diagnostic output; include traceability verification in acceptance test cases
rows.evidence: Suggested diagnostic output must be traceable to configured rules, input parameter values, applicable thresholds, and rule version used at the time of generation.
rows.reason: Traceability is critical for regulatory compliance, clinical validation, and liability protection; acceptance criteria requires traceability but implementation details and verification process are not defined

rows.deliverable: Diagnostic suggestion display
rows.target: Appropriate physician-review language clarifying suggestions are for clinical support not substitute for physician judgment
rows.clause: The tool must display appropriate physician-review language clarifying that diagnostic suggestions are for clinical support and not a substitute for physician judgment.
rows.valueAtRisk: Inadequate disclaimers create material liability risk if tool is used for clinical decision-making
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA clinical stakeholders to approve disclaimer language
rows.recommendedAction: Draft prominent disclaimer language for ClientDBA clinical and legal review; display disclaimer on every screen showing diagnostic suggestions; obtain written approval of disclaimer language from ClientDBA clinical and legal stakeholders; include disclaimer verification in acceptance criteria
rows.evidence: The tool must display appropriate physician-review language clarifying that diagnostic suggestions are for clinical support and not a substitute for physician judgment.
rows.reason: Disclaimer is critical for liability protection but specific language, placement, and approval process are not defined; inadequate or unapproved disclaimers create material liability risk

rows.deliverable: Milestone deliverables
rows.target: Resolved or approved workaround before milestone acceptance
rows.clause: Critical and high-severity defects mutually agreed as blocking release shall be resolved or have an approved workaround before milestone acceptance.
rows.valueAtRisk: Lack of clear defect severity and blocking criteria may cause acceptance disputes
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to agree defect severity and blocking criteria
rows.recommendedAction: Define defect severity levels and blocking criteria during M1 discovery; establish defect triage and resolution process; document mutually agreed blocking defects before milestone acceptance testing
rows.evidence: Critical and high-severity defects mutually agreed as blocking release shall be resolved or have an approved workaround before milestone acceptance.
rows.reason: Defect resolution is acceptance criteria but severity levels, blocking criteria, and mutual agreement process are not defined; may cause disputes over whether defects block acceptance

rows.deliverable: Each milestone
rows.measurement: From delivery of milestone to written conformance/non-conformance
rows.clause: Within ten (10) business days from delivery of each milestone, ClientDBA shall complete user acceptance testing for the relevant milestone as per the acceptance criteria mutually agreed between the parties and provide written conformance/acceptance or non-conformance for the same.
rows.valueAtRisk: Deemed acceptance after 10 business days may result in acceptance of deliverables with undetected defects
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to complete UAT and provide written acceptance or non-conformance within 10 business days
rows.remedy: Deemed acceptance if ClientDBA fails to provide conformance or non-conformance within 10 business days
rows.recommendedAction: Establish clear UAT process with test case coverage, defect logging, and acceptance sign-off documentation; provide ClientDBA with UAT test cases and acceptance checklist at milestone delivery; track acceptance period and send reminder before deemed acceptance date
rows.evidence: Within ten (10) business days from delivery of each milestone, ClientDBA shall complete user acceptance testing for the relevant milestone as per the acceptance criteria mutually agreed between the parties and provide written conformance/acceptance or non-conformance for the same. If ClientDBA fails to provide such conformance or non-conformance within the Acceptance Period, the milestone shall be deemed accepted by ClientDBA.
rows.reason: 10 business days may be insufficient for thorough UAT of complex AI diagnostic tool; deemed acceptance mechanism may result in acceptance of deliverables with undetected defects; no process for extending acceptance period if critical issues are found late in testing

rows.deliverable: Clinical rule owners, physician SMEs, product owner, security contact, infrastructure contact, UAT users
rows.target: Available for workshops, reviews, approvals, milestone acceptance
rows.clause: Clinical rule owners, physician SMEs, product owner, security contact, infrastructure contact, and UAT users shall be made available for workshops, reviews, approvals, and milestone acceptance.
rows.valueAtRisk: Lack of stakeholder availability may delay milestones and trigger additional fees
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to make stakeholders available for workshops, reviews, approvals, milestone acceptance
rows.remedy: Delays in approvals may impact delivery dates and fees
rows.recommendedAction: Obtain ClientDBA commitment to stakeholder availability with defined response times for reviews and approvals; establish escalation process for stakeholder unavailability; track all stakeholder requests and response times to document delays
rows.evidence: Clinical rule owners, physician SMEs, product owner, security contact, infrastructure contact, and UAT users shall be made available for workshops, reviews, approvals, and milestone acceptance.
rows.reason: Stakeholder availability is critical dependency but no availability commitments, response times, or escalation process are defined; stakeholder unavailability may cascade to milestone delays and additional fees

rows.deliverable: Approved blood test parameter list, reference ranges, sample anonymized datasets, clinical-rule documentation, diagnostic categories, relevant disclaimers
rows.clause: ClientDBA shall provide approved blood test parameter list, reference ranges, sample anonymized datasets, clinical-rule documentation, diagnostic categories, and relevant disclaimers.
rows.valueAtRisk: Delays in clinical documentation provision may delay milestones and trigger additional fees
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA provision of approved parameter list, reference ranges, datasets, clinical-rule documentation, diagnostic categories, disclaimers
rows.remedy: Delays in approvals, sample data may impact delivery dates and fees
rows.recommendedAction: Define specific clinical documentation delivery dates tied to milestone schedule; establish documentation quality and completeness criteria; obtain ClientDBA commitment to provide documentation at least 10 business days before each milestone due date
rows.evidence: ClientDBA shall provide approved blood test parameter list, reference ranges, sample anonymized datasets, clinical-rule documentation, diagnostic categories, and relevant disclaimers.
rows.reason: Clinical documentation provision is critical dependency but no delivery dates or quality criteria are specified; delays in documentation provision may cascade to milestone delays and additional fees

rows.deliverable: Development/test environments, application access, test credentials, API documentation, data dictionaries, third-party licenses
rows.clause: ClientDBA shall provide development/test environments, application access, test credentials, API documentation, data dictionaries, and third-party licenses, if applicable.
rows.valueAtRisk: Delays in environment or license provision may delay milestones and trigger additional fees
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA provision of environments, access, credentials, API documentation, data dictionaries, third-party licenses
rows.remedy: Delays in environments, licenses, access may impact delivery dates and fees
rows.recommendedAction: Define specific environment and license delivery dates tied to milestone schedule; establish environment provisioning process with defined turnaround time; track all environment and access requests and delays
rows.evidence: ClientDBA shall provide development/test environments, application access, test credentials, API documentation, data dictionaries, and third-party licenses, if applicable.
rows.reason: Environment and license provision is critical dependency but no delivery dates or turnaround times are defined; delays in environment or license provision may cascade to milestone delays and additional fees

rows.deliverable: Approvals, sample data, environments, licenses, access, security reviews, clinical validation
rows.clause: Delays in approvals, sample data, environments, licenses, access, security reviews, or clinical validation may impact delivery dates and fees.
rows.valueAtRisk: Delays in ClientDBA approvals or validation may trigger schedule revisions and additional fees
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA timely approvals, sample data, environments, licenses, access, security reviews, clinical validation
rows.remedy: Delays may impact delivery dates and fees
rows.recommendedAction: Define approval and validation turnaround times for each dependency type; establish escalation process for delayed approvals; track all approval requests and turnaround times to document delays
rows.evidence: Delays in approvals, sample data, environments, licenses, access, security reviews, or clinical validation may impact delivery dates and fees.
rows.reason: Approval and validation delays may impact delivery dates and fees but no turnaround times or escalation process are defined; lack of clear approval process may cause disputes over delay attribution

rows.deliverable: Changes in scope, deliverables, acceptance criteria, assumptions, dependencies, timelines
rows.target: Written change order or amendment signed by both parties
rows.clause: The parties may introduce changes in scope of Services, deliverables, acceptance criteria, assumptions, dependencies, and timelines through a written change order or amendment signed by both parties.
rows.valueAtRisk: Lack of clear change request process may cause scope creep and disputes over additional fees
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to follow change management process for scope changes
rows.recommendedAction: Establish clear change request process with impact assessment, cost estimate, timeline impact, and approval workflow; use Annexure A-1 change request template; track all change requests and approvals
rows.evidence: The parties may introduce changes in scope of Services, deliverables, acceptance criteria, assumptions, dependencies, and timelines through a written change order or amendment signed by both parties.
rows.reason: Change management process requires written change order but detailed process, turnaround time, and approval authority are not defined; lack of clear process may cause delays in processing change requests

rows.deliverable: Additional blood test parameters, diagnostic categories, integrations, advanced AI/ML model development, production support, security certification, regulatory support
rows.clause: Any additional blood test parameters, diagnostic categories, integrations, advanced AI/ML model development, production support, security certification, or regulatory support not expressly included in this SOW shall be treated as a change request.
rows.valueAtRisk: Production utility may require multiple change requests and additional fees not budgeted by ClientDBA
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to request out-of-scope items through change control
rows.exclusions: Additional parameters, categories, integrations, advanced AI/ML, production support, security certification, regulatory support excluded unless added through change request
rows.recommendedAction: Provide ClientDBA with cost and timeline estimates for common out-of-scope items during M1 discovery; clarify which items are likely needed for production deployment; initiate change requests early to avoid deployment delays
rows.evidence: Any additional blood test parameters, diagnostic categories, integrations, advanced AI/ML model development, production support, security certification, or regulatory support not expressly included in this SOW shall be treated as a change request.
rows.reason: Many items likely needed for production deployment are explicitly excluded and require change requests; ClientDBA may not have budgeted for these additional costs; lack of early planning may delay production deployment

rows.deliverable: Milestone invoices
rows.target: Per Agreement payment terms, or 30 days if not specified
rows.clause: Payments under this SOW shall be made as per the payment terms of the Agreement. If the Agreement does not specify payment terms, undisputed invoices shall be payable within thirty (30) days from receipt of invoice.
rows.valueAtRisk: Payment delays may impact cash flow
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to pay undisputed invoices per Agreement terms or within 30 days
rows.recommendedAction: Confirm Agreement payment terms; establish invoice dispute resolution process; track invoice submission and payment dates
rows.evidence: Payments under this SOW shall be made as per the payment terms of the Agreement. If the Agreement does not specify payment terms, undisputed invoices shall be payable within thirty (30) days from receipt of invoice.
rows.reason: Payment terms reference Agreement which is not uploaded; if Agreement does not specify payment terms, default is 30 days which is standard; low risk

rows.deliverable: SOW Fees
rows.clause: The SOW Fees are exclusive of applicable taxes, which shall be charged separately, if applicable.
rows.valueAtRisk: Tax costs may increase total cost to ClientDBA
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to pay applicable taxes
rows.recommendedAction: Clarify applicable tax rates and tax treatment; provide ClientDBA with estimated tax costs; confirm tax exemption status if applicable
rows.evidence: The SOW Fees are exclusive of applicable taxes, which shall be charged separately, if applicable.
rows.reason: Taxes are excluded from fixed price which is standard; tax costs may increase total cost to ClientDBA but typically not material; low risk

rows.deliverable: Travel, lodging, boarding, local conveyance
rows.measurement: Reimbursed at actuals per Agreement or written approval
rows.clause: The current scope does not include travel or onsite services. Any out-of-pocket expenses, including travel, lodging, boarding, and local conveyance, if requested and pre-approved by ClientDBA, shall be reimbursed at actuals in accordance with the Agreement or a mutually agreed written approval.
rows.valueAtRisk: Travel expenses may increase total cost if onsite services are requested
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to pre-approve any travel expenses
rows.exclusions: Travel and onsite services excluded from current scope
rows.recommendedAction: Clarify whether onsite services are anticipated; if yes, provide travel cost estimate and obtain pre-approval; establish expense approval and reimbursement process
rows.evidence: The current scope does not include travel or onsite services. Any out-of-pocket expenses, including travel, lodging, boarding, and local conveyance, if requested and pre-approved by ClientDBA, shall be reimbursed at actuals in accordance with the Agreement or a mutually agreed written approval.
rows.reason: Travel and onsite services are excluded which is clear; reimbursement requires pre-approval which protects both parties; low risk

rows.deliverable: Offshore resources
rows.clause: Offshore resources will be based out of India or any other place of work assigned by CTIND under this project as the base location. The current scope of Services does not include onsite services. Any change to this assumption will require the parties to follow the change management process.
rows.valueAtRisk: Offshore delivery model is clear; change to onsite requires change management
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to accept offshore delivery model
rows.remedy: Change management process for onsite services
rows.recommendedAction: Confirm ClientDBA acceptance of offshore delivery model; establish communication and collaboration protocols for offshore team; clarify time zone coverage and meeting schedules
rows.evidence: Offshore resources will be based out of India or any other place of work assigned by CTIND under this project as the base location. The current scope of Services does not include onsite services. Any change to this assumption will require the parties to follow the change management process.
rows.reason: Offshore delivery model is clearly stated; change to onsite requires change management which is appropriate; low risk

rows.deliverable: Pre-existing CTIND frameworks, reusable code, templates, rules-engine patterns, test automation scripts, DevOps scripts, AI prompt/configuration templates, audit trail patterns, quality engineering accelerators
rows.measurement: Royalty-free license to ClientDBA for utilization of project deliverables
rows.clause: Project deliverables may include pre-existing CTIND frameworks, reusable code components, templates, rules-engine patterns, test automation scripts, DevOps scripts, AI prompt/configuration templates, audit trail patterns, and quality engineering accelerators. CTIND shall retain ownership of all rights, title, and interests, including intellectual property rights, in such Accelerators. CTIND grants ClientDBA a royalty-free license in Accelerators solely for utilization of project deliverables under this SOW.
rows.valueAtRisk: ClientDBA receives only a license to use Accelerators, not ownership; license is limited to utilization of project deliverables which may restrict future use
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to accept license terms for Accelerators
rows.recommendedAction: Clarify scope of royalty-free license including whether ClientDBA can modify, enhance, or reuse Accelerators in other projects; clarify whether license survives termination; document which components are Accelerators vs custom work product
rows.evidence: CTIND shall retain ownership of all rights, title, and interests, including intellectual property rights, in such Accelerators. CTIND grants ClientDBA a royalty-free license in Accelerators solely for utilization of project deliverables under this SOW.
rows.reason: ClientDBA receives only a license to use Accelerators, not ownership; license scope is limited to utilization of project deliverables which may restrict ClientDBA ability to modify, enhance, or reuse components; unclear whether license survives termination

rows.deliverable: Cloud hosting, database services, AI/LLM APIs or platforms, rules-engine libraries, identity provider, secure file transfer tools, test management tools, code repository, DevOps pipeline, monitoring tools
rows.target: ClientDBA to procure at its cost
rows.clause: In case any third-party licenses are required for performance of Services, ClientDBA shall procure such third-party licenses at its cost and provide necessary access to CTIND. ClientDBA shall ensure that it has valid licenses to use such third-party software, is authorized to provide access to CTIND, and has adopted necessary internal processes to comply with license conditions.
rows.valueAtRisk: Third-party license costs may be substantial and are excluded from fixed price; ClientDBA responsible for license compliance which creates legal risk if not managed properly
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to procure third-party licenses, provide access to CTIND, ensure valid licenses, ensure authorization to provide access, comply with license conditions
rows.exclusions: Third-party license costs excluded from fixed price
rows.recommendedAction: Provide ClientDBA with detailed list of required third-party tools and estimated license costs; obtain ClientDBA procurement commitment and budget approval; clarify license compliance requirements and responsibilities; establish process for CTIND to request access to third-party tools
rows.evidence: In case any third-party licenses are required for performance of Services, ClientDBA shall procure such third-party licenses at its cost and provide necessary access to CTIND. ClientDBA shall ensure that it has valid licenses to use such third-party software, is authorized to provide access to CTIND, and has adopted necessary internal processes to comply with license conditions.
rows.reason: Third-party license costs are excluded from fixed price and may be substantial for AI/LLM APIs, cloud hosting, and other tools; ClientDBA is responsible for license compliance which creates legal risk if ClientDBA does not have proper processes; lack of cost transparency may cause budget overruns

rows.deliverable: Services performed up to termination
rows.target: All services performed up to effective date of termination
rows.measurement: Fees for accepted milestones, work in progress, pre-approved investments, third-party costs
rows.clause: CTIND shall be entitled to payment for all services performed up to the effective date of termination including fees for any accepted milestones, work in progress, and any pre-approved investments and third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of ClientDBA.
rows.valueAtRisk: Work in progress valuation may be disputed; pre-approved investments and third-party costs may be substantial
rows.customerDependency: ClientDBA to pay for services performed up to termination

boardSummary.statement: Tool excludes medical diagnosis, regulatory approval, and clinical validation; ClientDBA owns patient safety responsibility
SaMD classification, HIPAA/GDPR advisory, and penetration testing excluded; compliance risk remains with ClientDBA
AI/LLM APIs, cloud infrastructure, security tools excluded from USD 275K; may materially increase total cost
Clinical approvals, data provision, stakeholder availability critical; delays trigger schedule revisions and additional fees
Managed services, defect support, security patches excluded; operational continuity risk after 31-Dec-2026 handover

proceedIf.statement: Obtain written confirmation from ClientDBA clinical and legal stakeholders acknowledging responsibility for clinical validation, regulatory compliance, and patient safety before starting work
Provide ClientDBA with estimated third-party cost breakdown for AI/LLM APIs, cloud, security tools; obtain budget approval and procurement commitment before M3
Clarify whether tool requires SaMD/medical device classification; if yes, engage regulatory consultant and adjust scope/timeline/budget accordingly
Obtain ClientDBA written commitment to stakeholder availability, clinical documentation delivery dates, and approval turnaround times tied to milestone schedule
Establish post-production support agreement covering defect remediation, security patches, and operational support before M5 handover

renegotiate.statement: Define formal clinical approval process with review cycle, approval authority, sign-off documentation, and turnaround time for all medical logic and diagnostic text
Establish clear process for documenting ClientDBA-caused delays with date, dependency, impact, and approval before invoking schedule revision or additional fees
Extend acceptance period from 10 to 15 business days for complex milestones M3 and M4 to allow thorough UAT of AI diagnostic tool
Clarify scope of royalty-free license for Accelerators including modification, enhancement, reuse rights, and whether license survives termination
Clarify production deployment plan and whether EHR/LIS integration is required; if yes, include integration in scope or initiate change request with cost/timeline estimate

financialNotes.statement: USD 275,000 fixed price for 5-month engagement (01-Aug-2026 to 31-Dec-2026) payable across five milestones upon acceptance or deemed acceptance
Fixed price with milestone-based invoicing; CTIND owns budget, scope, and delivery schedule; entitled to additional fees if ClientDBA causes delays

Estimated USD 137,500 High risk (clinical liability, regulatory compliance, clinical approval delays), USD 110,000 Medium risk (dependencies, third-party costs, acceptance), USD 27,500 Low risk (administrative)
AI/LLM APIs, cloud infrastructure, security tools, monitoring tools excluded from USD 275K; ClientDBA responsible for procurement at its cost; may materially increase total cost of ownership
CTIND entitled to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees through change management if milestone delivery delayed due to ClientDBA-caused delays; no cap on additional fees
Per Master Services Agreement dated 01-Aug-2026 (not uploaded); if Agreement does not specify payment terms, undisputed invoices payable within 30 days from receipt

revenueControls.title: Milestone acceptance tracking
revenueControls.detail: Track milestone delivery dates, acceptance testing completion, and written acceptance/non-conformance within 10 business days; invoke deemed acceptance if ClientDBA fails to respond

revenueControls.title: Dependency delay documentation
revenueControls.detail: Document all ClientDBA dependency requests (stakeholder availability, clinical documentation, environment access, approvals) with date, response time, and impact on milestone schedule to support additional fee claims

revenueControls.title: Change request tracking
revenueControls.detail: Track all out-of-scope requests (additional parameters, integrations, advanced AI/ML, production support, security certification) and process through formal change management with cost and timeline impact

revenueControls.title: Third-party cost tracking
revenueControls.detail: Track all third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of ClientDBA (cloud, AI/LLM APIs, security tools) with pre-approval documentation to support reimbursement claims

revenueControls.title: Clinical approval audit trail
revenueControls.detail: Maintain audit trail of all clinical approvals (medical logic, rule thresholds, clinical labels, disclaimers, diagnostic text) with approver name, title, date, and sign-off documentation

commercialBaseline.interpretation: Fixed price for 5-month engagement; excludes third-party costs, travel, taxes, and out-of-scope items
CTIND owns budget, scope, and delivery schedule; entitled to additional fees if ClientDBA causes delays; consistent with EM4 classification
Five milestone payments upon acceptance or deemed acceptance; payment terms per Master Services Agreement or 30 days if not specified
ClientDBA responsible for procuring and funding cloud infrastructure, AI/LLM APIs, security tools, monitoring tools at its cost; may materially increase total cost
CTIND entitled to charge additional fees through change management if ClientDBA causes delays; no cap on additional fees
CTIND entitled to payment for accepted milestones, work in progress, pre-approved investments, and third-party costs incurred up to termination

leakageScenarios.impact: Milestone deemed accepted after 10 business days if ClientDBA fails to respond; may result in acceptance of deliverables with undetected defects; invoice triggered without quality validation
leakageScenarios.control: Track acceptance period for each milestone; send reminder to ClientDBA 5 business days before deemed acceptance date; escalate if UAT not started
leakageScenarios.evidence: Milestone delivery confirmation, UAT status reports, acceptance period tracking log, deemed acceptance notifications

leakageScenarios.impact: Milestone delays attributable to ClientDBA (stakeholder unavailability, approval delays, data delays, environment delays) not documented; unable to support schedule revision or additional fee claims
leakageScenarios.control: Document all ClientDBA dependency requests with date, expected response date, actual response date, and impact on milestone schedule; obtain ClientDBA acknowledgment of delays before invoking additional fees
leakageScenarios.evidence: Dependency request log, delay impact assessments, ClientDBA delay acknowledgments, change requests for schedule revision and additional fees

leakageScenarios.scenario: Out-of-scope work performed without change request
leakageScenarios.impact: Additional parameters, integrations, advanced AI/ML features, production support, or security certification performed without formal change request; unable to recover costs
leakageScenarios.control: Identify all out-of-scope requests during requirements and design phases; process through formal change management with cost and timeline impact before starting work; obtain signed change order
leakageScenarios.evidence: Change request log, change impact assessments, signed change orders, out-of-scope work tracking

leakageScenarios.impact: Cloud, AI/LLM API, security tool, or monitoring tool costs incurred for ClientDBA without pre-approval; unable to recover costs
leakageScenarios.control: Provide ClientDBA with estimated third-party cost breakdown; obtain written pre-approval before incurring costs; track all third-party costs with pre-approval documentation
leakageScenarios.evidence: Third-party cost estimates, pre-approval emails/documents, third-party invoices, cost tracking log

leakageScenarios.scenario: Work in progress valuation dispute at termination
leakageScenarios.impact: Contract terminated before milestone acceptance; work in progress valuation disputed; payment delayed or reduced
leakageScenarios.control: Establish clear work in progress valuation method (e.g. percentage of milestone fee based on effort expended); track effort and progress for each milestone; document work in progress with evidence
leakageScenarios.evidence: Effort tracking logs, work in progress reports, deliverable evidence, termination settlement calculation

penaltyExposure.High: No service credits or penalties stated in uploaded contract; however, material clinical liability, regulatory compliance, and patient safety risks exist if tool is used for clinical decision-making without proper validation, regulatory approval, and clinical sign-off
penaltyExposure.Medium: No service credits or penalties stated in uploaded contract; however, delays in ClientDBA dependencies (clinical approvals, data provision, stakeholder availability) may trigger schedule revisions and additional fees with no stated cap
penaltyExposure.Low: No service credits or penalties stated in uploaded contract

recos.contract: Section 3.2 - Out of Scope (Clinical Validation and Regulatory Compliance)
recos.action: Add explicit written acknowledgment from ClientDBA clinical and legal stakeholders of responsibility for clinical validation, regulatory compliance, and patient safety; require ClientDBA to provide evidence of qualified clinical resources and regulatory compliance plan before starting work
recos.original: The tool shall not be treated as a standalone medical diagnosis, treatment prescription, or automated clinical decision-making system unless expressly agreed in a separate written agreement. Final diagnosis, patient management, care decisions, regulatory approvals, medical content approval, clinical validation, and clinical safety sign-off shall remain the responsibility of ClientDBA and its qualified clinical stakeholders.
recos.rewrite: The tool shall not be treated as a standalone medical diagnosis, treatment prescription, or automated clinical decision-making system unless expressly agreed in a separate written agreement. Final diagnosis, patient management, care decisions, regulatory approvals, medical content approval, clinical validation, and clinical safety sign-off shall remain the responsibility of ClientDBA and its qualified clinical stakeholders. ClientDBA shall provide CTIND with written acknowledgment from ClientDBA's authorized clinical and legal stakeholders confirming (a) ClientDBA's responsibility for clinical validation, regulatory compliance, and patient safety, (b) availability of qualified clinical resources to perform validation, and (c) regulatory compliance plan, before CTIND commences work under this SOW. ClientDBA shall indemnify and hold harmless CTIND from any claims, liabilities, or damages arising from ClientDBA's use of the tool for clinical decision-making, regulatory non-compliance, or patient safety incidents.

recos.action: Define formal clinical approval process with review cycle, approval authority, sign-off documentation, and turnaround time; require written clinical approval for all medical logic, rule thresholds, clinical labels, disclaimers, and diagnostic text before each milestone acceptance
recos.original: ClientDBA clinical stakeholders shall review and approve all medical logic, rule thresholds, clinical labels, disclaimers, and user-facing suggested diagnostic text before production use.
recos.rewrite: ClientDBA clinical stakeholders shall review and approve all medical logic, rule thresholds, clinical labels, disclaimers, and user-facing suggested diagnostic text before production use. ClientDBA shall designate authorized clinical approvers with name, title, and approval authority within 5 business days of SOW effective date. CTIND shall submit medical logic, rule thresholds, clinical labels, disclaimers, and diagnostic text for clinical review at each milestone. ClientDBA clinical approvers shall complete review and provide written approval or non-approval with specific feedback within 10 business days of submission. CTIND shall maintain audit trail of all clinical approvals including approver name, title, date, and sign-off documentation. No milestone shall be deemed accepted until all required clinical approvals for that milestone are obtained in writing.

recos.action: Draft prominent disclaimer language for ClientDBA clinical and legal review; require written approval of disclaimer language; display disclaimer on every screen showing diagnostic suggestions; include disclaimer verification in acceptance criteria
recos.original: The tool must display appropriate physician-review language clarifying that diagnostic suggestions are for clinical support and not a substitute for physician judgment.
recos.rewrite: The tool must display appropriate physician-review language clarifying that diagnostic suggestions are for clinical support and not a substitute for physician judgment. CTIND shall draft disclaimer language and submit to ClientDBA clinical and legal stakeholders for review and written approval within 10 business days of M2 completion. Approved disclaimer language shall be displayed prominently on every screen showing diagnostic suggestions, in a font size and color that ensures visibility and readability. Disclaimer shall include at minimum: (a) diagnostic suggestions are for clinical support only, (b) suggestions are not a substitute for physician judgment, (c) final diagnosis and treatment decisions remain the responsibility of the qualified physician, and (d) tool has not been independently validated or certified as a medical device. Milestone acceptance criteria shall include verification that approved disclaimer language is displayed on all relevant screens.

recos.action: Establish clear process for documenting ClientDBA-caused delays with date, dependency, impact, and approval; require ClientDBA acknowledgment of delay attribution before invoking schedule revision or additional fees; cap additional fees at 25% of affected milestone fee
recos.original: If delivery of any milestone is delayed due to causes attributable to ClientDBA, CTIND will be entitled to revise its delivery schedule and charge additional fees to ClientDBA through the change management process.
recos.rewrite: If delivery of any milestone is delayed due to causes attributable to ClientDBA, CTIND will be entitled to revise its delivery schedule and charge additional fees to ClientDBA through the change management process. CTIND shall document each ClientDBA-caused delay with (a) date of dependency request, (b) description of dependency, (c) expected response date, (d) actual response date, (e) delay duration, and (f) impact on milestone schedule. CTIND shall provide ClientDBA with delay impact assessment within 5 business days of delay occurrence. ClientDBA shall review and provide written acknowledgment or dispute of delay attribution within 5 business days of receiving impact assessment. If ClientDBA disputes delay attribution, parties shall meet to resolve dispute within 5 business days. CTIND may invoke schedule revision and additional fees only after obtaining ClientDBA written acknowledgment of delay attribution. Additional fees for ClientDBA-caused delays shall not exceed 25% of the affected milestone fee unless mutually agreed in writing.

recos.action: Extend acceptance period from 10 to 15 business days for complex milestones M3 and M4; establish process for extending acceptance period if critical issues are found late in testing; clarify that deemed acceptance does not waive ClientDBA right to claim defects discovered post-acceptance
recos.original: Within ten (10) business days from delivery of each milestone, ClientDBA shall complete user acceptance testing for the relevant milestone as per the acceptance criteria mutually agreed between the parties and provide written conformance/acceptance or non-conformance for the same. If ClientDBA fails to provide such conformance or non-conformance within the Acceptance Period, the milestone shall be deemed accepted by ClientDBA.
recos.rewrite: Within ten (10) business days from delivery of milestones M1, M2, and M5, and within fifteen (15) business days from delivery of milestones M3 and M4, ClientDBA shall complete user acceptance testing for the relevant milestone as per the acceptance criteria mutually agreed between the parties and provide written conformance/acceptance or non-conformance for the same. If ClientDBA identifies critical or high-severity defects within 2 business days before the end of the Acceptance Period, ClientDBA may request a one-time extension of up to 5 business days to complete remediation testing, which CTIND shall not unreasonably withhold. If ClientDBA fails to provide such conformance or non-conformance within the Acceptance Period (including any approved extension), the milestone shall be deemed accepted by ClientDBA. Deemed acceptance does not waive ClientDBA's right to claim defects discovered within 30 days post-acceptance that were not reasonably discoverable during the Acceptance Period, provided ClientDBA provides CTIND with reasonable opportunity to remediate such defects.

recos.contract: Section 14 - Accelerators / CTIND IP (IP License Scope)
recos.action: Clarify scope of royalty-free license for Accelerators including modification, enhancement, and reuse rights; clarify whether license survives termination; document which components are Accelerators vs custom work product
recos.original: CTIND shall retain ownership of all rights, title, and interests, including intellectual property rights, in such Accelerators. CTIND grants ClientDBA a royalty-free license in Accelerators solely for utilization of project deliverables under this SOW.
recos.rewrite: CTIND shall retain ownership of all rights, title, and interests, including intellectual property rights, in such Accelerators. CTIND grants ClientDBA a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, non-exclusive license to use, modify, and enhance Accelerators solely for ClientDBA's internal business purposes in connection with the project deliverables under this SOW. ClientDBA may not sublicense, distribute, or commercialize Accelerators to third parties. License survives termination of this SOW. CTIND shall provide ClientDBA with a list of Accelerators included in each milestone deliverable, clearly distinguishing Accelerators from custom work product developed specifically for ClientDBA. Custom work product developed specifically for ClientDBA (excluding Accelerators) shall be owned by ClientDBA upon full payment of the applicable milestone fee.

recos.contract: Section 15 - Third-Party Tools / Materials (Third-Party License Costs)
recos.action: Require CTIND to provide ClientDBA with detailed list of required third-party tools and estimated license costs during M1 discovery; obtain ClientDBA budget approval and procurement commitment before M3; establish process for CTIND to request access to third-party tools with defined turnaround time
recos.original: In case any third-party licenses are required for performance of Services, ClientDBA shall procure such third-party licenses at its cost and provide necessary access to CTIND.
recos.rewrite: CTIND shall provide ClientDBA with a detailed list of required third-party tools and estimated license costs during M1 discovery, including cloud hosting, database services, AI/LLM APIs or platforms, rules-engine libraries, identity provider, secure file transfer tools, test management tools, code repository, DevOps pipeline, and monitoring tools. ClientDBA shall review the list and provide written budget approval and procurement commitment within 10 business days of receiving the list. ClientDBA shall procure approved third-party licenses at its cost and provide necessary access to CTIND within 10 business days of CTIND's written access request. If ClientDBA fails to provide access within the specified timeframe, CTIND shall be entitled to revise the delivery schedule through the change management process. ClientDBA shall ensure that it has valid licenses to use such third-party software, is authorized to provide access to CTIND, and has adopted necessary internal processes to comply with license conditions.

recos.contract: Section 16 - Payment Entitlement (Work in Progress Valuation)
recos.action: Define clear work in progress valuation method; establish process for documenting work in progress with evidence; clarify that pre-approved investments and third-party costs are limited to those with written pre-approval
recos.original: CTIND shall be entitled to payment for all services performed up to the effective date of termination including fees for any accepted milestones, work in progress, and any pre-approved investments and third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of ClientDBA.
recos.rewrite: CTIND shall be entitled to payment for all services performed up to the effective date of termination including (a) fees for any accepted milestones, (b) work in progress for milestones not yet accepted, valued at the percentage of the milestone fee corresponding to the percentage of effort expended as documented in CTIND's effort tracking logs and work in progress reports, and (c) pre-approved investments and third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of ClientDBA, limited to those with written pre-approval from ClientDBA. CTIND shall provide ClientDBA with termination settlement calculation within 15 business days of termination effective date, including detailed breakdown of accepted milestone fees, work in progress valuation with supporting evidence, and pre-approved investments and third-party costs with supporting documentation. ClientDBA shall review and provide written acceptance or dispute of termination settlement within 15 business days of receiving calculation. If ClientDBA disputes the calculation, parties shall meet to resolve dispute within 10 business days.

assumptions.assumption: All values extracted from uploaded contract text only; no external information used; Master Services Agreement dated 01-Aug-2026 referenced but not uploaded; payment terms, termination terms, and other Agreement provisions not verified

EM4 – Fixed Price classification based on fixed-price SOW with committed milestones, deliverables, acceptance criteria, and budget ownership by CTIND; CTIND owns delivery schedule and is entitled to revise schedule and charge additional fees if ClientDBA causes delays; consistent with fixed-price pricing model

Contract value USD 275,000 is total fixed price for 5-month engagement; no monthly fee stated; contractValueNumeric set to 275000 for KPI display; third-party costs, travel, taxes, and out-of-scope items excluded from contract value

Value at risk allocated High 50% (USD 137,500), Medium 40% (USD 110,000), Low 10% (USD 27,500) based on risk severity and potential impact; allocation is directional estimate for visualization purposes
Commercial baseline derived from pricing model, payment terms, third-party cost exclusions, additional fee entitlement, and termination payment provisions stated in uploaded contract

Revenue controls focus on milestone acceptance tracking, dependency delay documentation, change request tracking, third-party cost tracking, and clinical approval audit trail to protect CTIND's ability to invoice milestones and claim additional fees

Leakage scenarios focus on deemed acceptance without thorough UAT, untracked ClientDBA-caused delays, out-of-scope work without change request, third-party costs without pre-approval, and work in progress valuation disputes

Confidence scores reflect strength of evidence in uploaded contract; lower confidence when evidence is weak, ambiguous, or depends on unuploaded Master Services Agreement; higher confidence when evidence is explicit and unambiguous

Page numbers extracted from [[PAGE n]] markers in uploaded PDF; pageNo set to page number from nearest preceding marker above the clause; evidence quotes are verbatim from contract text to enable page location verification

Master Services Agreement dated 01-Aug-2026 referenced but not uploaded; payment terms, termination terms, IP ownership, liability, indemnity, confidentiality, and other Agreement provisions not verified; manual validation required

Contract describes AI diagnostic tool processing blood test parameters; no patient identifiers (MRN, date of birth, diagnoses, SSN) visible in uploaded contract; however, tool will process patient data in production; ClientDBA responsible for HIPAA/GDPR compliance, data security, and patient privacy

No service-level commitments, SLAs, service credits, or penalties stated in uploaded contract; milestone delivery dates are target dates, not committed SLAs with penalties; CTIND entitled to revise schedule if ClientDBA causes delays

Contract text appears to be clean PDF extraction; no obvious OCR errors detected; signature blocks present but actual signatures not visible; recommend manual validation of Master Services Agreement, signature execution, and third-party license agreements
